sections.paragraph:
κύμινον, ου, τό (a word of Phoenician origin, cp. Hb. כַּמֹּן; Hippocr., Theophr. et al.; PTebt 112, 13; 314, 19; PFay 101 I, 9; Is 28:25, 27) cuminum cyminum (Zohary, Plants 88), cum(m)in. The tiny fruits (‘seeds’) of the cumin, an annual herb, were tithed despite their slight value Mt 23:23 (to show how relative this slightness of value was cp. Sb. 7667 [320 a.d.], a contract for the delivery of cumin to be paid for in advance).—Schürer II 263; Billerb. I 933.—M-M.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hb. Hb. = Hebrew

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

PFay PFay = Fayûm Towns and Their Papyri—List 4

Zohary Zohary = MZ., various works—List 6

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

Billerb. Billerb. = HLStrack and PBillerbeck, Kommentar z. NT aus Talmud u. Midrasch—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 575
